channel.title: Errlsappnottrusted on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions
channel.description: Recent content in Errlsappnottrusted on ErrorCode — Error Code Reference & Solutions

item.title: [Solution] macOS Error -10830 (kLSDataUnavailable) — Application Not Trusted
item.description:
macOS Error -10830 (kLSDataUnavailable) — Application Not Trusted

Error -10830 (kLSDataUnavailable / errLSAppNotTrusted) indicates that Launch Services has determined the application is not trusted by the system. This error occurs when macOS security mechanisms, such as Gatekeeper or code signing verification, have flagged an application as untrusted. The application may fail to launch, open files, or register with Launch Services until the trust issue is resolved.

Common Causes

  • The application is not signed by a recognized developer certificate
  • Gatekeeper has blocked the application from running
  • The application’s code signature is invalid or expired
  • The application was downloaded from the internet and hasn’t been approved by the user
  • The developer certificate has been revoked by Apple
  • The application has been modified after signing, invalidating its signature

How to Fix Error -10830

1. Override Gatekeeper for the Application

Approve the application through System Preferences or Terminal:

item.title: [Solution] macOS Error -10836 (kLSDataUnavailable) — Application Not Trusted
item.description:
macOS Error -10836 (kLSDataUnavailable) — Application Not Trusted

Error -10836 (kLSDataUnavailable / errLSAppNotTrusted) indicates that Launch Services cannot verify the trustworthiness of the application. Similar to error -10830, this error occurs when macOS security mechanisms flag an application as untrusted. Error -10836 specifically indicates that the trust verification process itself failed or returned an indeterminate result, often due to missing or expired certificates in the trust chain.

Common Causes

  • The application’s trust chain cannot be fully verified
  • Intermediate or root certificates are missing from the system keychain
  • The application was signed but the signing certificate has expired
  • The trust evaluation timed out due to network issues (OCSP/CRL check)
  • The application has been modified after signing, invalidating the trust
  • macOS security policy prevents the application from being trusted

How to Fix Error -10836

1. Verify the Application Signature

Check the full signature and trust chain:
